full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Designing a failure mode that links to my Task 1.2 assumptions.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: my assumption 1 was that data must be centered. how do i design a synthetic dataset where this assumption is violated and offset-free svm clearly fails while standard svm works
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: I chose specific cluster centers (8, 0.5) and (8, -0.5) with noise=1.0 myself. The LLM gave the general direction.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Suggested placing both clusters on the same side of origin far from x=0 and separated on y-axis. Also told me NOT to apply StandardScaler for this experiment, which I would have done by habit.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Drew a sketch to check that no line through origin can separate the clusters correctly.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Checking that the failure actually fails (not accidentally correct).
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: wait actually couldn't a line through origin at a nearly horizontal angle still separate points with y>0 from y<0? would offset-free svm actually fail here or might it accidentally get good accuracy
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: After this I made the clusters overlap by adding noise=1.0, which forces a clear failure. Change is my own design decision.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Pointed out yes — in a clean separation case a horizontal-ish plane might accidentally work. Suggested adding noise to make the clusters overlap on the y-axis so the boundary position matters. I then re-ran and confirmed the failure was clear.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Ran the code with clean vs noisy clusters and saw the difference in accuracy.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: the exam asks for one sentence suggesting a fix to the failure mode. whats the simplest fix without reintroducing b
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: I chose centering-as-preprocessing over the alternative of adding b back. Sentence is my own writing.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Offered two options: (1) mandatory centering step built in, or (2) learn an offset via a separate unconstrained optimization. I went with option 1 as simpler.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Exam says no implementation required so just confirmed the suggestion is reasonable.

top_5_prompts.prompt: how do i design a dataset where centering assumption is violated and offset-free svm clearly fails
top_5_prompts.why_important: The failure mode must connect to Task 1.2 assumptions. Getting this right closes the loop the grader is looking for.

top_5_prompts.prompt: couldn't a line through origin still separate y>0 from y<0 accidentally? would it actually fail?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Good sanity check. Saved me from submitting an experiment where the 'failure' accidentally succeeds, which would have been embarrassing.

top_5_prompts.prompt: whats the simplest fix without reintroducing b
top_5_prompts.why_important: The one-sentence fix is part of the marking. Having a thoughtful answer here shows I understood what caused the failure.

student declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for the indicated task.
